name: Roques del Barrufet and Mirador de Alforja Loop
name_original: Subida al Mirador de Alforja — circular desde la Ermita Verge de la Riera
description: Hike to Mirador de Alforja from Ermita Verge de la Riera, a moderate 5.0-mile loop with panoramic views and historical sites.
region_intro: Hiking around Les Borges Del Camp provides access to diverse natural landscapes, situated at the foothills of the Sierra de la Mussara and the Prades Mountains. The region features a blend of agricultural plains, including hazelnut orchards and olive groves, alongside more elevated terrain. The Riera d'Alforja River Valley also contributes to the local topography, offering varied routes for exploration. Best hiking trails around Les Borges Del Camp The most popular hiking route is Roques del Barrufet and Mirador de Alforja Loop, a 5.0 miles (8.1 km) trail that takes 2 hours 23 minutes to complete, offering panoramic views and historical sites. Another top favourite among local hikers is Hiking loop from les Borges del Camp, a moderate 6.8 miles (11.0 km) path that traverses varied terrain through agricultural plains and foothills. Local hikers also love the Hermitage of Our Lady of the Riera loop from les Borges del Camp, a 6.5 miles (10.5 km) trail leading through agricultural landscapes to the modernist Hermitage of Our Lady of the Riera, often completed in about 3 hours 7 minutes. Hiking around Les Borges Del Camp is defined by agricultural plains, hazelnut orchards, and mountainous terrain. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging treks. The routes in Les Borges Del Camp are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 500 reviews. More than 3000 hikers have used komoot to explore Les Borges Del Camp's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Les Borges Del Camp?
A: There are over 14 hiking routes around Les Borges Del Camp, offering a variety of experiences. These include 6 easy trails and 8 moderate trails, catering to different fitness levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around Les Borges Del Camp?
A: The terrain around Les Borges Del Camp is quite diverse. You'll find a blend of agricultural plains, including extensive hazelnut orchards and olive groves, alongside more elevated and mountainous areas at the foothills of the Sierra de la Mussara and the Prades Mountains. Some routes also follow the Riera d'Alforja River Valley.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Les Borges Del Camp offers several easy hiking options perfect for beginners or families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17511001" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roques del Barrufet – Alforja Viewpoint loop from les Borges del Camp&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 6.3 km route that provides pleasant views without significant challenge.

Q: What are the best times of year to go hiking in Les Borges Del Camp?
A: The region is generally pleasant for hiking during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Spring brings blooming orchards, while autumn offers beautiful colors in the agricultural landscapes. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended, and winters are generally mild, making it possible to hike year-round.

Q: Are there any hikes with great viewpoints in Les Borges Del Camp?
A: Absolutely. Many trails offer stunning panoramic views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1306534172/roques-del-barrufet-and-mirador-de-alforja-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roques del Barrufet and Mirador de Alforja Loop&lt;/a&gt; is known for its scenic vistas. You can also find viewpoints offering expansive views of the Sierra de Montsant, Prades Mountains, and the Camp de Tarragona region. Consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/782537" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint over the Cingles de Patxeco&lt;/a&gt; for impressive scenery.

Q: What historical or cultural sites can I visit while hiking?
A: The area is rich in history. You can hike to the modernist &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10419924" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Our Lady of the Riera&lt;/a&gt;, located a short distance from the town. The historic Siurana Village, with its Romanesque church and magnificent views, is also a significant draw nearby. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4614860" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Throne of Siurana&lt;/a&gt; is a notable natural monument with historical ties.

Q: Are there any natural wonders to explore on the trails?
A: Yes, the region boasts impressive natural features. One notable natural wonder is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/725611" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Els Avencs de la Febró&lt;/a&gt;, an open crevice in the Mussara mountain range. You can also discover the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/880629" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pont de Goi Natural Arch&lt;/a&gt;, another fascinating geological formation.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Les Borges Del Camp?
A: Many trails in Les Borges Del Camp are suitable for dogs, allowing you to enjoy the natural beauty with your canine companion. It's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in agricultural areas or near wildlife, and to follow local regulations regarding pet access.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available?
A: Yes, many of the trails around Les Borges Del Camp are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14891183" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiking loop from les Borges del Camp&lt;/a&gt;, which traverses varied terrain, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10420161" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alforja Viewpoint – Rocabruna (539 m) loop from les Borges del Camp&lt;/a&gt;, a longer route for those seeking a challenge.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Les Borges Del Camp?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 500 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, from tranquil hazelnut orchards to panoramic mountain views, and the opportunity to combine nature exploration with visits to historical sites like the Hermitage of Our Lady of the Riera.

Q: What is the typical duration for a moderate hike in this area?
A: Moderate hikes in Les Borges Del Camp typically range from 2.5 to 4.5 hours. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1306534172/roques-del-barrufet-and-mirador-de-alforja-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roques del Barrufet and Mirador de Alforja Loop&lt;/a&gt; takes approximately 2 hours 23 minutes, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10419924" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Our Lady of the Riera loop&lt;/a&gt; is often completed in about 3 hours 7 minutes.
tips: Spectacular hermitage not only because of its shape but also because of its location (close to Borges del Camp) and which represents a very well-preserved modernist sanctuary.
On clear days you can enjoy a good view of Alforja.
On clear days you can enjoy a good view of the Prades Mountains

faqs.question: Where does the Ascent to Mirador de Alforja trail start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail is a circular route starting from the Hermitage of Our Lady of the Riera (Ermita Verge de la Riera) in Alforja. Alforja is a picturesque medieval town, and parking options are typically available within the town or near the hermitage itself, though it's always good to check local signage upon arrival.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this hike, and what fitness level is required?
faqs.answer: This hike is rated as moderate. It involves a significant altitude gain to reach the Alforja Viewpoint, making it a bit more challenging than some other routes in the area. Hikers should have good fitness and be sure-footed, especially on ancient paths.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain and scenery can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: The route utilizes a network of ancient paths, traversing an incomparable historical and natural environment. You'll pass through mountains and olive groves, ascend via the Collado de Alforja, and return along the Porrera path parallel to the Alforja stream. The scenery culminates in extensive panoramic views from the Alforja Viewpoint.

faqs.question: What notable landmarks or viewpoints will I encounter along the way?
faqs.answer: Key highlights include the starting point at the Hermitage of Our Lady of the Riera, the remains of the Toribio ice house, and the ultimate destination, the Alforja Viewpoint, which offers stunning vistas, including a viewpoint overlooking the Prades Mountains. At the summit, you can also observe a small sculpture by Jassans.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail or out-and-back?
faqs.answer: This is a circular route, meaning you will start and end at the same point without retracing your steps.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike to Mirador de Alforja?
faqs.answer: Given the significant altitude gain and exposure at the viewpoint, spring and autumn are generally the most pleasant seasons for this hike. The weather is typically milder, offering comfortable hiking conditions and clear views. Summer can be very hot, and winter might bring colder temperatures at higher elevations.

faqs.question: Is the Ascent to Mirador de Alforja trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail is generally accessible, specific rules regarding dogs (e.g., leash requirements) can vary. It's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers. Please ensure your dog is fit enough for a moderate hike with significant elevation gain.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this hike?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the Ascent to Mirador de Alforja trail. It is part of the 'Rutas Alforja' project, which promotes free access to the area's natural and cultural heritage.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with other local paths. Specifically, it covers about 19% of the 'Les Borges del Camp-Puigcerver' trail and 13% of the 'Camí de la Ermita' trail.

faqs.question: Are there any specific tips for making the most of this hike?
faqs.answer: To fully appreciate the historical and natural environment, take your time exploring the ancient paths and the remains of the Toribio ice house. The Alforja Viewpoint at 915 meters is the highest point in the municipality, so plan your visit on a clear day for the most expansive and stunning panoramic views.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and elevation gain, it's advisable to bring plenty of water, sturdy hiking boots for sure-footedness on varied terrain, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and snacks. A camera is also recommended to capture the spectacular views from the Mirador de Alforja.

waypoints.name: Hermitage of Our Lady of the Riera
